clusters.title: Scientists identify an ancient brainstem network that acts as an attentional selection engine
clusters.summary: Deep within the brainstem, an ancient group of neurons acts as an attentional selection engine. A new study shows these cells are essential for ignoring distractions, offering new insights into how both animals and humans focus.

items.title: Scientists identify an ancient brainstem network that acts as an attentional selection engine
items.description: Deep within the brainstem, an ancient group of neurons acts as an attentional selection engine. A new study shows these cells are essential for ignoring distractions, offering new insights into how both animals and humans focus.

items.title: Scientists discover ancient brain cells that help block distractions
items.description: Scientists have discovered a tiny group of neurons in an ancient brain region that acts like a built-in focus filter, helping the brain ignore distractions and zero in on what matters most. When researchers temporarily switched off these neurons in mice, the animals became unusually distractible—similar to what is seen in ADHD—but regained normal focus as soon as the neurons were reactivated.

clusters.title: Researchers block key protein that helps Parkinson’s spread through the brain
clusters.summary: A newly identified protein called GPNMB may play a major role in helping Parkinson’s disease spread through the brain. Researchers discovered that immune cells release the protein in response to damaged neurons, creating a vicious cycle that speeds up brain cell degeneration. In early experiments, antibodies that blocked GPNMB stopped the toxic process from spreading between cells.

items.title: Researchers block key protein that helps Parkinson’s spread through the brain

clusters.title: Investigational J-valve system shows favorable 1-year outcomes for severe aortic regurgitation
clusters.summary: New research published in JSCAI and presented at New York Valves 2026 reports favorable one-year outcomes in patients with symptomatic severe aortic regurgitation following treatment with the investigational J-Valve Transfemoral System.

items.title: Investigational J-valve system shows favorable 1-year outcomes for severe aortic regurgitation
items.description: New research published in JSCAI and presented at New York Valves 2026 reports favorable one-year outcomes in patients with symptomatic severe aortic regurgitation following treatment with the investigational J-Valve Transfemoral System.

clusters.title: Common plastic chemical linked to lifelong anxiety in new study
clusters.summary: Exposure to a common plastic chemical before and shortly after birth may have lasting effects on behavior. Researchers found that male rats exposed early in life to DEHP—a plasticizer used in products ranging from medical devices to toys—showed significantly higher anxiety as adults, even long after exposure had ended. The animals were more hesitant to explore open spaces and spent more time frozen in place, classic signs of anxiety in rodents.

items.title: Common plastic chemical linked to lifelong anxiety in new study
items.description: Exposure to a common plastic chemical before and shortly after birth may have lasting effects on behavior. Researchers found that male rats exposed early in life to DEHP—a plasticizer used in products ranging from medical devices to toys—showed significantly higher anxiety as adults, even long after exposure had ended. The animals were more hesitant to explore open spaces and spent more time frozen in place, classic signs of anxiety in rodents.

clusters.title: New study explores potential cross-species spread of chronic wasting disease
clusters.summary: A new study found that chronic wasting disease can sometimes spread silently, with infectious prions present even in animals that show no symptoms. While there is no confirmed human risk, researchers say the disease’s ability to evolve and spread across species warrants close attention.

items.title: New study explores potential cross-species spread of chronic wasting disease

While there is no confirmed human risk, researchers say the disease’s ability to evolve and spread across species warrants close attention.

clusters.title: The secret behind smoother, better-tasting protein shakes
clusters.summary: A new whey protein manufacturing technique could make protein shakes taste better and feel smoother in the mouth. Researchers traced unwanted bitterness to concentrated minerals and found a way to remove them without sacrificing the improved texture.

items.title: The secret behind smoother, better-tasting protein shakes
items.description: A new whey protein manufacturing technique could make protein shakes taste better and feel smoother in the mouth. Researchers traced unwanted bitterness to concentrated minerals and found a way to remove them without sacrificing the improved texture.

clusters.title: Scientists say most people need more protein than current guidelines suggest
clusters.summary: A new review suggests that doing more exercise and eating more protein than current minimum recommendations may help people stay stronger, sharper, and more independent as they age. The goal isn't building a beach body—it's extending healthspan and maintaining the ability to fully enjoy life for decades longer.

items.title: Scientists say most people need more protein than current guidelines suggest

The goal isn't building a beach body—it's extending healthspan and maintaining the ability to fully enjoy life for decades longer.

clusters.title: Alcohol absorption nearly doubles after bariatric surgery, raising long-term misuse risk
clusters.summary: Your body absorbs alcohol much more rapidly after bariatric surgery. Patients need to know this when they choose the kind of surgery they will have. "Bariatric surgery can come with a price.

items.title: Alcohol absorption nearly doubles after bariatric surgery, raising long-term misuse risk
items.description: Your body absorbs alcohol much more rapidly after bariatric surgery. Patients need to know this when they choose the kind of surgery they will have. "Bariatric surgery can come with a price. Patients have a significantly higher risk of developing alcohol problems than if they did not undergo surgery," said Magnus Strømmen, a researcher at the Center for Obesity Research at St. Olavs Hospital and a Ph.D. research fellow at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU).

clusters.title: Slapping an “AI-generated” label on political messages doesn’t stop people from believing them
clusters.summary: Policymakers hope that forcing AI companies to label their generated text will help curb political misinformation. However, a new study reveals that humans are just as persuaded by policy arguments labeled "written by AI" as those written by humans.

items.title: Slapping an “AI-generated” label on political messages doesn’t stop people from believing them
items.description: Policymakers hope that forcing AI companies to label their generated text will help curb political misinformation. However, a new study reveals that humans are just as persuaded by policy arguments labeled "written by AI" as those written by humans.

clusters.title: Developing a new methodology to identify biomarkers for male infertility
clusters.summary: Researchers from the EHU-University of the Basque Country have developed a robust protocol to detect the molecules involved in the metabolic reactions of sperm. The new method enables a detailed analysis even with small samples and allows the detection of the largest number of potential biomarkers identified to date; this advance could help diagnose male infertility and develop future clinical solutions. The paper is published in the journal Cells.

clusters.title: This emerging treatment is helping people avoid knee replacement surgery
clusters.summary: A minimally invasive treatment called GAE is helping people with chronic knee pain get back to gardening, cycling, and other activities without undergoing knee replacement surgery. Early studies suggest the procedure can provide years of relief by reducing inflammation inside the joint.

items.title: This emerging treatment is helping people avoid knee replacement surgery
items.description: A minimally invasive treatment called GAE is helping people with chronic knee pain get back to gardening, cycling, and other activities without undergoing knee replacement surgery. Early studies suggest the procedure can provide years of relief by reducing inflammation inside the joint.

clusters.title: 10 surprising ways diabetes and dementia are connected
clusters.summary: Diabetes and dementia appear to be closely intertwined, with each condition potentially influencing the other. Problems with insulin and glucose can affect the brain’s energy supply, increase inflammation, and damage blood vessels linked to memory loss. Researchers are also finding that some popular diabetes medications may lower dementia risk.

items.title: 10 surprising ways diabetes and dementia are connected
items.description: Diabetes and dementia appear to be closely intertwined, with each condition potentially influencing the other. Problems with insulin and glucose can affect the brain’s energy supply, increase inflammation, and damage blood vessels linked to memory loss. Researchers are also finding that some popular diabetes medications may lower dementia risk. These discoveries are opening new possibilities for protecting brain health as people age.

clusters.title: A common vitamin could help fight one of the deadliest brain cancers
clusters.summary: A clinical trial is exploring whether high doses of vitamin B3 could give patients with glioblastoma a better chance against the aggressive brain cancer. Scientists found that niacin may help revive immune cells that tumors shut down, allowing them to attack cancer more effectively. Early results have been promising, with patients showing significantly better progression-free survival than expected.

items.title: A common vitamin could help fight one of the deadliest brain cancers

Early results have been promising, with patients showing significantly better progression-free survival than expected.

clusters.title: One common fat may fuel type 2 diabetes while another helps fight it
clusters.summary: Not all fats affect your body the same way. Researchers found that palmitic acid, a saturated fat common in many foods, may contribute to insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes by triggering inflammation, toxic fat buildup, and cellular stress. In contrast, oleic acid—the heart-healthy fat abundant in olive oil—appears to help protect insulin function and may even counter some of the harmful effects of palmitic acid.

clusters.title: People who embrace national and global identities report higher life satisfaction
clusters.summary: Do national pride and global citizenship conflict? Research analyzing over 100,000 survey responses shows that while they slightly temper one another, identifying strongly with both your country and the world is associated with greater well-being.

items.title: People who embrace national and global identities report higher life satisfaction
items.description: Do national pride and global citizenship conflict? Research analyzing over 100,000 survey responses shows that while they slightly temper one another, identifying strongly with both your country and the world is associated with greater well-being.

clusters.title: New research sheds light on why women tend to generate more creative ideas during the ovulatory phase
clusters.summary: A recent study suggests that women tend to generate more original ideas during ovulation. Researchers found this temporary creative boost is actually a natural side effect of unconscious physiological arousal rather than an evolutionary mating signal.

items.title: New research sheds light on why women tend to generate more creative ideas during the ovulatory phase
items.description: A recent study suggests that women tend to generate more original ideas during ovulation. Researchers found this temporary creative boost is actually a natural side effect of unconscious physiological arousal rather than an evolutionary mating signal.

clusters.title: People taking GLP-1 weight loss drugs like Ozempic started moving less
clusters.summary: People taking popular weight-loss drugs such as Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, and Zepbound may be losing pounds, but they could also be moving less. Researchers analyzing Fitbit data found that daily step counts and exercise levels dropped after people started these medications, despite successful weight loss. Because the drugs can reduce muscle mass along with fat, the decline in physical activity raises concerns about preserving strength and long-term health.

items.title: People taking GLP-1 weight loss drugs like Ozempic started moving less
items.description: People taking popular weight-loss drugs such as Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, and Zepbound may be losing pounds, but they could also be moving less. clusters.title: This common vitamin deficiency can mimic normal aging
clusters.summary: Vitamin B12 is needed in microscopic amounts, but a shortage can have major effects on health and energy. The vitamin was first linked to a lifesaving liver treatment for pernicious anemia nearly 100 years ago. Today, researchers are finding that B12 may also help keep cellular powerhouses called mitochondria functioning properly.

items.title: This common vitamin deficiency can mimic normal aging
items.description: Vitamin B12 is needed in microscopic amounts, but a shortage can have major effects on health and energy. The vitamin was first linked to a lifesaving liver treatment for pernicious anemia nearly 100 years ago. Today, researchers are finding that B12 may also help keep cellular powerhouses called mitochondria functioning properly. This could explain why some people experience fatigue and brain fog even before traditional signs of deficiency show up.

clusters.title: Geometric neural 'map' may help bilingual brains navigate between languages
clusters.summary: Anyone who speaks more than one language knows the feeling of expressing the same thought through entirely different linguistic lenses. A new study by researchers at Rice University and Baylor College of Medicine reveals that the key to this translation ability is a shared geometric map of neural responses in the hippocampus.

items.title: Geometric neural 'map' may help bilingual brains navigate between languages

clusters.title: Think you're eating healthy? You may be missing this heart-protecting nutrient
clusters.summary: Eating five servings of fruits and vegetables may not be enough if you're missing foods rich in flavanols, a group of compounds linked to better heart health. Researchers found that choices like blackberries, plums, apples, broad beans, cherries, and green tea can dramatically increase flavanol intake.
